title: The Witness
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A historian discovers a single-word discrepancy in an immutable archive, unraveling a quarter-century conspiracy to edit the past.
long_description:
Every moment is recorded. The Witness Archive stores every public event, every conversation, every transaction. When a question of fact arises, you consult the Archive. Truth is never in doubt.

When historian Dr. Priya Sundar compares two records of the same event — the signing of the Unity Charter — she finds a discrepancy: a phrase that appears in one recording but not the other. The difference is small — one word — but impossible in a system where records are immutable. Someone has edited the past.

Priya must discover who, how, and most importantly, what else they changed — before the Archive’s integrity unravels entirely. Her investigation leads her through a labyrinth of political conspiracy, a buried massacre, and the truth about the Union’s founding — a truth that could tear society apart.
